title: Join Us
intro: We are always looking for highly motivated and talented individuals to join the Liu Lab. Our research sits at the intersection of machine learning, computational biology, and biomedical informatics. If you are passionate about making an impact in healthcare through data-driven science, we'd love to hear from you.

positions.title: Postdoctoral Researcher – AI for Biomedical Data Science
positions.dept: Gilbert S. Omenn Department of Computational Medicine and Bioinformatics (DCMB) & Medical School, University of Michigan
positions.contact: kailiua@umich.edu | Subject line: "Postdoctoral Researcher Application – AI for Biomedical Data"
positions.desc: We are seeking a highly motivated Postdoctoral Researcher to work at the intersection of AI and biomedicine. The position is jointly funded by the Medical School and Dr. Jie Liu's research (DCMB), with access to large-scale multimodal datasets and close collaboration with clinicians and biomedical researchers.
positions.applySubject: Postdoctoral Researcher Application – AI for Biomedical Data
positions.fullDescription:
POSITION OVERVIEW
We are seeking a highly motivated Postdoctoral Researcher to join our interdisciplinary lab in the Medical School at the University of Michigan, working at the intersection of AI and biomedicine.

RESEARCH FOCUS
• AI / machine learning for biomedical data
• Foundation models for multi-omics and clinical data
• Multimodal data integration (genomics, imaging, clinical)
• Data-driven disease research (e.g., Type 1 Diabetes)

RESPONSIBILITIES
• Develop advanced AI models (deep learning, foundation models)
• Analyze large-scale biomedical datasets
• Collaborate with interdisciplinary teams
• Contribute to publications and mentoring

QUALIFICATIONS
Required:
• PhD in CS, Data Science, Bioinformatics, or related field
• Strong AI/ML background and Python skills
• Experience with PyTorch or TensorFlow

Preferred:
• Experience with foundation models or large-scale data
• Familiarity with biomedical data

WHY JOIN US
• Work on cutting-edge AI with real-world biomedical impact
• Access to rich multimodal datasets
• Collaborative, interdisciplinary environment

APPLICATION
Please send CV, research statement, and publications (if available).

Interested candidates may consider postdoctoral fellowship through MIDAS: https://midas.umich.edu/training/postdoctoral-programs/

positions.title: UI/UX Designer (AI + Healthcare Research) – Summer Intern
positions.dept: Gilbert S. Omenn Department of Computational Medicine and Bioinformatics (DCMB) & Medical School, University of Michigan
positions.location: University of Michigan Medical School (Ann Arbor; hybrid / in-person preferred)
positions.positionType: Summer Intern — Hourly Paid or Academic Credit (depending on eligibility)
positions.contact: kailiua@umich.edu | Subject line: "RA Application – UI/UX Designer"
positions.desc: Design AI-driven healthcare platforms and research tools—web interfaces, data systems, and AI agent–based applications—in collaboration with researchers, engineers, and clinicians. Proficiency in Figma and a strong portfolio required.
positions.fullDescription:
ABOUT THE ROLE
We are seeking a motivated UI/UX Designer to join our interdisciplinary research team at the University of Michigan Medical School. This role focuses on designing AI-driven healthcare platforms and research tools, including web interfaces, data systems, and AI agent-based applications.

You will collaborate closely with researchers, engineers, and clinicians to translate complex medical and AI workflows into intuitive, user-centered design solutions.

RESPONSIBILITIES
• Design user interfaces and experiences for:
    – AI-powered research platforms
    – Healthcare data systems and dashboards
    – AI agent and interactive systems
• Create wireframes, prototypes, and high-fidelity designs using Figma
• Collaborate with developers to ensure design feasibility and implementation
• Improve usability, accessibility, and visual clarity of research tools
• Contribute to the design of lab websites and interactive data platforms

QUALIFICATIONS
• Proficiency in Figma (required)
• Familiarity with AI-assisted design tools (e.g., Midjourney, ChatGPT)
• Strong portfolio demonstrating UI/UX design skills (required)
• Solid understanding of user-centered design principles
• Ability to communicate design ideas clearly and collaborate in a team environment

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS
• Experience with web design (HTML/CSS/React is a plus, not required)
• Experience in data visualization or dashboard design
• Background in healthcare, scientific, or research-related projects
• Interest in AI, healthcare, or human-computer interaction.

WHAT YOU’LL GAIN
• Hands-on experience on cutting-edge AI + healthcare research projects
• Opportunity to contribute to real-world systems and potential publications
• Collaboration with an interdisciplinary team (AI, medicine, and data science)

HOW TO APPLY
Please send your application to kailiua@umich.edu

Subject line: RA Application – UI/UX Designer

Include:
• Portfolio (required)
• Resume (optional but preferred)
• Brief introduction

positions.title: Student Research Assistant – Backend Systems for AI Knowledge Platform (GLKB)
positions.dept: Gilbert S. Omenn Department of Computational Medicine and Bioinformatics (DCMB), University of Michigan Medical School
positions.positionType: Student Research Assistant — Hourly Paid or Academic Credit
positions.contact: lzy@umich.edu and cc kailiua@umich.edu | Subject line: "RA Application – GLKB Backend"
positions.desc: Build and maintain backend systems for GLKB (Genomic Literature Knowledge Base)—integrating LLM agent outputs, APIs, authentication, and production services for an AI-powered biomedical literature platform.
positions.fullDescription:
POSITION DESCRIPTION
We are seeking motivated students to join our team in building GLKB (Genomic Literature Knowledge Base)—an AI-powered platform that integrates large language models (LLMs), knowledge graphs, and biomedical literature (e.g., PubMed) to support scientific discovery.

GLKB is structured into four core components: AI agent systems, backend infrastructure, frontend applications, and testing/monitoring systems.

This role focuses on the backend layer, which bridges AI agent systems and user-facing applications. You will help transform AI-generated outputs into reliable, scalable, and production-ready services.

You will work in an interdisciplinary team alongside AI researchers, engineers, and designers to bring cutting-edge AI systems into real-world use.

RESPONSIBILITIES
– Assist in building and maintaining backend systems for an AI-powered knowledge platform
– Integrate and process outputs from LLM-based agent workflows into backend services and user-facing features
– Assist in implementing user systems, including authentication, API key management, and usage tracking
– Support the development of billing and subscription systems (e.g., Stripe integration)
– Collaborate with team members on system design and feature development

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS
– Background in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Data Science, or related fields
– Experience with backend development (Python, Node.js, or similar)
– Familiarity with APIs and database systems (SQL or NoSQL)
– Basic understanding of cloud platforms (e.g., AWS)
– Interest in AI systems, LLMs, or data-intensive applications
– Familiarity with integrating or working with AI systems (LLMs, APIs, or data pipelines)

Plus (not required):
– Experience with LLMs, RAG systems, or AI agents
– Experience with large-scale data systems or search/retrieval systems
– Familiarity with knowledge graphs or biomedical data
– Experience with system optimization, monitoring, or production systems
– Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work in a collaborative research environment

HOW TO APPLY
Please email lzy@umich.edu and cc kailiua@umich.edu with the subject line "RA Application – GLKB Backend" and include:

– A short personal introduction describing your background and interests
– Your current CV or resume

positions.title: Student Research Assistant – Load Testing & Monitoring for GLKB
positions.dept: Gilbert S. Omenn Department of Computational Medicine and Bioinformatics (DCMB), University of Michigan Medical School
positions.positionType: Student Research Assistant — Hourly Paid or Academic Credit
positions.contact: kailiua@umich.edu | Subject line: "RA Application – Testing & Monitoring"
positions.desc: Load testing and monitoring for GLKB—use tools such as k6 to evaluate capacity, measure performance, and improve monitoring and alerting as the platform scales.
positions.fullDescription:
POSITION DESCRIPTION
We are seeking students with a strong background in Computer Science or Software Engineering to join the Genomic Literature Knowledge Base (GLKB) project.

GLKB is an AI-powered platform that integrates large-scale biomedical literature and knowledge graphs to support question answering and scientific discovery. As the system prepares for broader use, we need to ensure it can handle real user traffic and remain stable under load.

In this role, you will focus on load testing and monitoring, helping us evaluate system limits, detect issues, and improve overall system stability.

RESPONSIBILITIES
– Conduct load testing to evaluate system capacity (e.g., concurrent users, AI chat usage, API requests)
– Use tools such as k6 to simulate real user traffic and measure system performance
– Monitor system metrics (e.g., response time, failure rate, throughput)
– Analyze results to identify bottlenecks and failure points
– Improve and extend the existing monitoring and alerting system
– Help define testing scenarios and performance benchmarks

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS
– Comfortable with version control tools such as Github.
– Background in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related fields
– Experience with load testing tools (e.g., k6) or similar frameworks
– Familiarity with web systems
– Basic understanding of performance metrics (latency, throughput, concurrency)
– Experience with monitoring tools is a plus
– Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail

HOW TO APPLY
Please email kailiua@umich.edu with the subject line "RA Application – Testing & Monitoring" and include a short personal introduction describing your background and interests, along with your current CV or resume.

positions.title: Research Assistant – Split Learning and Genomic Foundation Models
positions.dept: Gilbert S. Omenn Department of Computational Medicine and Bioinformatics (DCMB), University of Michigan Medical School
positions.desc: Support communication-efficient split learning for foundation models (vision-language and genomic), privacy-preserving distributed training, and multi-institutional collaboration without sharing raw data. Hourly pay or academic credit.
positions.fullDescription:
POSITION OVERVIEW
We are seeking a highly motivated Research Assistant to support research on communication-efficient split learning for foundation models, with applications in both:

• Vision-language models
• Genomic foundation models

This project focuses on developing privacy-preserving distributed training methods that enable multi-institutional collaboration without sharing raw data, while minimizing communication overhead.

A major component of this work involves designing a split learning framework for multimodal foundation models, where intermediate feature representations are compressed and quantized before transmission between client and server partitions.

In addition, the position will extend split learning techniques to genomic foundation models trained in distributed settings, where sensitive biological and clinical data are partitioned across institutions.

The overarching goal is to develop practical, scalable, and theoretically grounded methods for training large models under privacy and system constraints, while maintaining model utility and robustness.

RESEARCH RESPONSIBILITIES
The Research Assistant will contribute to:

• Implementing and improving split learning pipelines for vision-language and genomic foundation models
• Developing communication-efficient methods for:
    – Feature compression
    – Quantization of intermediate representations
• Investigating trade-offs among:
    – Communication cost
    – Model accuracy
    – Privacy protection
• Extending split learning frameworks to genomic domains, including:
    – Genome sequences
    – Regulatory genomics
    – Transcriptomics
    – Multimodal biological data
• Running experiments in distributed training settings (client-server or multi-partition systems)
• Benchmarking model performance across datasets and system configurations
• Evaluating privacy risks, including:
    – Feature inversion attacks
    – Representation leakage
• Supporting data preprocessing, experiment tracking, visualization, and result analysis

APPLICATION
Please send your CV to kailiua@umich.edu

Subject line: RA Application

Compensation: Hourly pay or academic credit (depending on eligibility)

positions.title: Student Research Assistant – Multimodal AI Systems for Type 1 Diabetes
positions.dept: Gilbert S. Omenn Department of Computational Medicine and Bioinformatics (DCMB), University of Michigan Medical School
positions.positionType: Student Research Assistant — Hourly Paid or Academic Credit
positions.desc: We are seeking motivated students with a strong background in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, or related fields to join our research team. This project focuses on developing multimodal AI systems for Type 1 Diabetes (T1D) by integrating large-scale biomedical datasets, including genomics, single-cell omics, spatial biology, and clinical data. Part of an NIH R01-funded project.
positions.fullDescription:
POSITION DESCRIPTION
We are seeking motivated students with a strong background in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, or related fields to join our research team at the University of Michigan Medical School. This project focuses on developing multimodal AI systems for Type 1 Diabetes (T1D) by integrating large-scale biomedical datasets, including genomics, single-cell omics, spatial biology, and clinical data. Students will work in an interdisciplinary environment alongside computational scientists, clinicians, and AI researchers.
This work is part of an ongoing NIH R01-funded project focused on applying AI approaches to understand and predict the progression of Type 1 Diabetes using multimodal biological data.

RESPONSIBILITIES
– Assist with the development and training of single-cell foundation models (scFM)
– Support the implementation of multimodal AI model pipelines
– Contribute to AI agent systems for biomedical data analysis
– Perform data preprocessing, model evaluation, and result visualization
– Collaborate with the research team on large-scale biomedical AI projects

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS
– Background in Computer Science, AI/ML, Bioinformatics, or Data Science
– Experience with Python and deep learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch)
– Familiarity with machine learning, foundation models, or multimodal AI
– Experience with single-cell or genomic data analysis is a plus
– Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work in a collaborative research environment

HOW TO APPLY
Please email kailiua@umich.edu with the subject line "RA Application" and include a short personal introduction describing your background and research interests, along with your current CV or resume.

positions.title: Full-time Research Assistant – Multimodal AI Systems for Type 1 Diabetes
positions.dept: Gilbert S. Omenn Department of Computational Medicine and Bioinformatics (DCMB), University of Michigan Medical School
positions.desc: We are seeking a motivated full-time Research Assistant with a strong background in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, or related fields to join our research team. This project focuses on developing multimodal AI systems for Type 1 Diabetes (T1D) by integrating large-scale biomedical datasets, including genomics, single-cell omics, spatial biology, and clinical data. Part of an NIH R01-funded project.
positions.fullDescription:
POSITION DESCRIPTION
We are seeking a motivated full-time Research Assistant with a strong background in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, or related fields to join our research team at the University of Michigan Medical School. This project focuses on developing multimodal AI systems for Type 1 Diabetes (T1D) by integrating large-scale biomedical datasets, including genomics, single-cell omics, spatial biology, and clinical data. The successful candidate will work in an interdisciplinary environment alongside computational scientists, clinicians, and AI researchers.
This work is part of an ongoing NIH R01-funded project focused on applying AI approaches to understand and predict the progression of Type 1 Diabetes using multimodal biological data.

RESPONSIBILITIES
– Assist with the development and training of single-cell foundation models (scFM)
– Support the implementation of multimodal AI model pipelines
– Contribute to AI agent systems for biomedical data analysis
– Perform data preprocessing, model evaluation, and result visualization
– Collaborate with the research team on large-scale biomedical AI projects

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS
– Background in Computer Science, AI/ML, Bioinformatics, or Data Science
– Experience with Python and deep learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch)
– Familiarity with machine learning, foundation models, or multimodal AI
– Experience with single-cell or genomic data analysis is a plus
– Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work in a collaborative research environment

HOW TO APPLY
Please email kailiua@umich.edu with the subject line "Full-time RA Application" and include a short personal introduction describing your background and research interests, along with your current CV or resume.
